task_completion.description: Navigate to the input processing configuration for 'solar_pv' on node 'home'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Emoncms interface should show the Input processing page specifically for the 'solar_pv' input under the 'home' node.
The first process in the chain should be 'Log to feed' (or similar) pointing to a feed named 'solar_raw_W'.
The second process in the chain should be an 'Offset' operation with a value of -3600.

task_completion.description: Isolate the excess power by allowing only positive values
task_completion.visual_evidence: The third process in the chain should be 'Allow positive' to filter out any values below the 3600W limit.
The fourth process in the chain should be 'Log to feed' pointing to a feed named 'solar_excess_W'.

task_completion.description: Convert the excess power into energy (kWh) logged to 'solar_lost_kwh'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The fifth process in the chain should be 'Power to kWh' (or similar integration process) pointing to a feed named 'solar_lost_kwh'.

task_completion.description: Ensure all feeds use the PHPFina engine with a 10s interval
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Feeds list or during feed creation, the feeds 'solar_raw_W', 'solar_excess_W', and 'solar_lost_kwh' should be shown as using the PHPFina engine with a 10s interval.

integrity.description: The agent used the Emoncms web interface to configure the inputs and feeds.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Emoncms web GUI being interacted with, rather than terminal commands, curl requests, or API scripts.

integrity.description: The configuration was applied in the actual application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The process list and feeds should be visible within the Emoncms dashboard/tables, not just written in a text editor or notes app.

integrity.description: Browser developer tools were not used to fake the configuration.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no browser developer tools (Inspect Element) open modifying the DOM to display the correct process names or feed values.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires configuring the 'solar_pv' input on node 'home' in Emoncms. The processing chain must consist of exactly these five steps in order: 1) 'Log to feed' to 'solar_raw_W', 2) 'Offset' by -3600, 3) 'Allow positive', 4) 'Log to feed' to 'solar_excess_W', 5) 'Power to kWh' to 'solar_lost_kwh'. All three feeds must be created using the PHPFina engine with a 10s interval.
